element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Turn-based battles with controlled difficulty scaling
Can freely recruit and swap party members; builds throughout game
Six base classes available; additional classes unlock in remakes
Progressive stat growth via XP; supports long-term progression
Stat-based equipment progression mirrors level advancement
Spells unlock via leveling; varies by class chosen
Gates access to certain NPCs, items, and quests; enables temporal puzzle-solving

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After initial setup (towns accessible in varying orders)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: World designed for player agency; backtracking required for progression
Mid-game milestone; enables character customization and advanced strategies
Optional gold acquisition mechanic; rewards speculation
Extends through entire game; structured around world-spanning destination
Increases in complexity and enemy density; requires party power development

typical_relief_triggers: Party recruitment and team composition adjustment
Successful class change for character customization
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters with story progression gated behind them
Party member KOs (represented as ghosts in NA version)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical exploration-combat-rest rhythm with escalating challenge tied to world progression; open-world pacing allows player agency over encounter difficulty through routing choices

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Branching paths and side areas accessible at taverns and through day/night variations; monster arena as optional side loop

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with controllable party composition and optional early recruitment of additional party members; starting equipment varies between JP (minimal) and NA (enhanced)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating dungeon complexity at Level 20 milestone; Dhama Temple class-change system enables strategic party respeccing to counter mid-game enemy types
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-difficulty endgame dungeons leading to Baramos encounter; requires optimized party builds and resource management
notable_spikes: Midgame class-change juncture (Level 20) introduces meta-progression pressure
Major boss encounters (Baramos implied as climactic spike)
Late-game dungeon density and enemy stat escalation

notable_level_patterns.description: Typical progression loop: reach town hub → acquire quests/items/party members → enter adjacent dungeon → defeat boss or gather resources → return to town → move to next area
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early-game town progressions leading to story dungeons

notable_level_patterns.description: Same spatial area presents different content, NPCs, and quests based on day/night state; encourages revisiting locations and adds temporal puzzle dimension
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Towns with time-locked NPCs and events; day/night-dependent quest access

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple towns and dungeons accessible in non-linear order; player chooses progression path; difficulty varies by route choice, encouraging exploration and experimentation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-game world hub where multiple dungeon routes available simultaneously

notable_level_patterns.description: Mandatory location at approximate game midpoint where class-change system unlocks; serves as difficulty valve and party customization checkpoint
